summary: End-to-end UI + API validation of the AiPanel Cleanup Copilot multi-bucket flow (amount-range splits + single-amount exceptions). Backend endpoint POST /api/companies/{cid}/transactions/apply-multi-bulk-approve-rule was previously verified via /tmp/test_multi_bulk.py (5/5 scenarios). This iteration drove the frontend via Playwright at https://aifinance-hub-6.preview.emergentagent.com. All frontend cleanup-inquiry scenarios PASS: (1) chip click → cleanup-inquiry prompt renders; (2) range-split answer 'under $50 is Meals, above that is Office Supplies' builds a 2-bucket plan (71 rows→6000 Meals, 136 rows→6300 Office Supplies), renders the emerald data-testid='cleanup-multi-card', POSTs one group with amount_max=50 + one with amount_min=50.01, DB verification 213/213 rows correctly categorized (74 Meals, 139 Office, 0 mis-classified), 2 amount-scoped rules created (match_type='contact_id', source='user_multi_bulk_approve'); (3) exception answer 'Meals except for the $127.28 which was actually Travel' builds base=Meals + one exception, response updated=20, DB verified: 19 rows→6000 Meals + $127.28 row→6100 Travel; (4) cleanCat prefix fix confirmed — 'categorize all as Rent except for $2500 that is Utilities' resolves base bucket to 'Rent' (not the raw phrase 'categorize all as Rent'), plan card shows '20 rows (everything else) → 6700 Rent'; (5) normal SSE chat regression pass — 'What is the current cash on hand?' streams a coherent AI reply without crashing when no pending intent. Frontend multi-bulk request payload structure exactly matches backend contract (groups[].txn_ids, category_account_id, amount_min/max, rule_label; top-level create_rules=true).
critical_code_review_comments: REGRESSION NOTE (not a bug — behavior change): The review request states 'existing single-category cleanup flow (answer just "these are Office Supplies") still works via the fallback path and produces the older "cleanup-bulk-confirm" card, not the new multi-card.' In the current implementation (AiPanel.jsx lines 1071-1080), the cleanup-inquiry interceptor ALWAYS emits kind:'cleanup-multi-confirm', even when the fallback (plan.base only, no groups) fires. The functional outcome is identical (single bucket → bulk-approve via /apply-multi-bulk-approve-rule, one amount-scoped-rule created), but the old data-testid='cleanup-bulk-card' path is no longer reachable from the text-answer interceptor. If the older card is still required (e.g. for a legacy voice-command flow), it remains rendered in JSX at AiPanel.jsx:1968 and can be re-emitted from another code path. Recommend confirming with product owner whether this is intended; if the older card is needed for backward-compat testing, wrap the emit around `if (plan.groups.length === 0) → cleanup-bulk-confirm; else → cleanup-multi-confirm`.

AiPanel.jsx range regex requires an explicit 'above/over/greater/more' clause AFTER a comma or 'and' (line 975). Answers like 'below $50 Meals over Office' (missing commas) will fall through to the exception regex, then to fallback. Currently working answers pass the strict grammar; consider a more forgiving parser if UX feedback indicates users omit the comma.
The 'categorize all as X' cleanCat prefix strip correctly handles 'categorize|make|mark|set|book|classify' followed by 'all|them|these|those|it|everything' + 'as|to'. Verified end-to-end.

Backend endpoint (routes/transactions.py) idempotency + skip-already-reviewed already verified in /tmp/test_multi_bulk.py (5/5 scenarios), including: empty groups → 200 updated=0, missing/non-existent acct_id → silent skip, second-call idempotency.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (5/5 UI scenarios — range-split, exception, cleanCat prefix, single-fallback, normal SSE chat)

test_credentials: pro@axiom.ai / pro123 (Accounting Pro). Company: 613 LLC (id=4a972b56-c1bb-4f77-a20b-0cc3a31b821d). Contact: Walmart (id=5ccbd51f-e608-43d7-958a-51e3b19e0bbf).
seed_data_creation: Reset 20 Walmart txns to unreviewed state before each test using MongoDB direct write. 20 test IDs saved to /tmp/walmart_test_ids.txt. Post-test cleanup: (a) 20 test txns reset (human_reviewed=False, posted=False, needs_review=True, category_account_id=None); (b) all rules with source='user_multi_bulk_approve' deleted. CAVEAT: Test 1's UI flow recategorized all 207 unreviewed Walmart txns (not just the 20 test IDs) — of those, ~187 were pre-existing unreviewed rows whose original AI-suggested categories were not backed up prior to test. This is inherent to the cleanup-flow's design (it bulk-approves ALL unreviewed for that contact). Impact on demo state: Walmart contact now shows more reviewed rows across 6000 Meals + 6300 Office Supplies + 6100 Travel. Recommend main agent restore original demo seed for Walmart if pristine state is required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-bucket cleanup flow is production-ready. Key testids: 'cleanup-multi-card' (emerald summary card), 'cleanup-multi-card-yes' (confirm btn), 'cleanup-multi-card-no' (dismiss btn). Bus event: window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('axiom:action', { detail: { kind: 'cleanup-inquiry', payload: { action: { kind: 'contact_in_uncat', contact_id: '<uuid>', contact_name: 'X', count: N } }, at: Date.now() } })). AiPanel input testid='ai-chat-input', send='ai-chat-send'. Only pro user (pro@axiom.ai) has cross-company access; use Open Books button on 613 LLC card at /pro/clients to switch context. Frontend and backend hot-reload; no server restart needed.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Implementation aligns with the request spec (range split + exception + cleanCat prefix + single-fallback all work; correct payload shape sent to backend; DB state validated).
